bio:
Parviz Davoodi served as the First Vice President of Iran from September 2005 to July 2009 under President [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad). An economist with a Ph.D. from Iowa State University, he was also a professor at [Shahid Beheshti University](/orgs/shahid-beheshti-university).

Davoodi's replacement in July 2009 triggered one of the most significant confrontations between Ahmadinejad and [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei). Ahmadinejad attempted to replace Davoodi with [Esfandiar Rahim Mashaei](/people/esfandiar-rahim-mashaei), but Khamenei publicly vetoed the appointment within days, forcing Ahmadinejad to back down. This episode marked a turning point in the Khamenei-Ahmadinejad relationship.

After leaving the vice presidency, Davoodi remained a member of the [Expediency Discernment Council](/orgs/expediency-discernment-council) until his death. He died in Tehran on 18 April 2024 at the age of 72.

sources.title: Former Iranian Vice President Davoudi Passes Away At 72

editorial_notes: Born 5 February 1952. PhD Economics, Iowa State University. Died 18 April 2024 in Tehran. Expediency Council member until death.
